guide.hero.title: LPM System Guide
guide.hero.sub: Everything you need to manage clients, sessions, academy students and courses — all in one place. Read through each section below to get up and running quickly.
guide.overview.title: Overview
guide.overview.lead: LPM is split into four main areas, each accessible from the left-hand sidebar. Use the navigation menu to move between them at any time.
guide.overview.dashboardDesc: Your weekly session grid. Track how much auditing time each staff member delivered.
guide.overview.pcsDesc: Manage client profiles, payment history, purchased hours and session records.
guide.overview.academyDesc: Record daily student visits, enrol students in courses, and print weekly attendance sheets.
guide.overview.adminDesc: Manage courses, staff permissions, and session approvals. Admin role required.
guide.overview.tip: Your role (Admin or regular staff) determines which pages you can see. If a page is missing from your menu, contact your administrator.
guide.dashboard.title: Dashboard
guide.dashboard.sub: Weekly session tracking for auditors and CS
guide.dashboard.step1text: Use the Prev / Next buttons at the top to move between weeks. The week always runs Thursday → Wednesday. Today’s week is shown by default.
guide.dashboard.step2text: Each column is a staff member. Each row is a day. The cell shows the total billed session time for that person on that day (h:mm). CS columns appear greyed — they count separately in the grand total.
guide.dashboard.step3text: If you are both a regular and solo auditor, a Solo toggle button appears at the top of your dashboard. Switch to Solo mode to record solo sessions separately from your regular auditing time.
guide.dashboard.step4text: Click the Print button to generate a PDF of the weekly grid, including a staff leaderboard and day-by-day statistics chart.
guide.dashboard.step5text: Below the grid you can manage the list of PCs assigned to you this week. Add or remove PCs from your personal list using the selector at the bottom of the card.
guide.pcs.title: PCs (Clients)
guide.pcs.step1text: Type a name or external ID in the Search... box at the top of the left panel. The list filters instantly.
guide.pcs.step2text: Each PC in the list shows a coloured badge — green means hours remaining, red means over-used. The list is sorted with lowest hours at the top so you can spot who needs a top-up quickly.
guide.pcs.add1text: Press the green New PC button. Fill in the required fields — First name and Last name are mandatory. If a person with the same name already exists an error will appear before saving.
guide.pcs.add2text: Add phone, email, date of birth, sex and origin. These can also be filled in later by clicking Edit on the PC’s detail card.
guide.pcs.pay1text: Select a PC, then click + Add Payment in the Payments card.
guide.pcs.pay2text: Auditing — enter hours bought and amount paid. These hours count toward the remaining hours balance. Course — select the course from the dropdown and enter the amount paid. The PC is automatically enrolled in the Academy course.
guide.pcs.pay3text: Registrar defaults to your own name — change it if someone else processed the payment. Referral is optional: set it if the PC was referred by someone.
guide.pcs.tip: Course payments automatically create an open enrollment in the Academy. You do not need to enrol the student manually.
guide.academy.title: Academy
guide.academy.sub: Daily attendance, courses and weekly reports
guide.academy.visit1text: Click the day pill at the top of the page (Mon, Tue, …). The pill turns green when that day has visits. You cannot add visits to future days.
guide.academy.visit2text: Type in the Search students… box. Matching names appear as chips below. Click a chip to add a visit for that student on the selected day.
guide.academy.visit3text: In the visit list on the right, click the trash icon next to a student’s chip to remove their visit for the selected day.
guide.academy.new1text: Press the New Student button in the top-right of the navigation bar.
guide.academy.new2text: First name, Last name and Course are all required. A person with the same full name cannot be added twice — the system will warn you. Select the Referral and Organization if known.
guide.academy.new3text: Saving the form automatically records a visit for the currently selected day, so you don’t need to add it separately.
guide.academy.course1text: In the weekly student table, click the book icon on any student row. A modal opens showing their active and completed courses.
guide.academy.course2text: Click Finish next to an active course, pick the finish date, then confirm. The course moves to the completed list and the student’s active course column clears.
guide.academy.course3text: Use the Add Course dropdown at the bottom of the modal. Choose the course, set the start date, and click Add.
guide.academy.print1text: The Print button (top-right) generates a PDF for the currently displayed week. The PDF includes the student attendance table, referral & org breakdowns, each student’s active course with visit count, and a per-course summary table.
guide.academy.print2text: The PDF opens or downloads automatically. No printer is needed — you can save it or share the file directly.
guide.academy.tip: The Active Course column shows the course name and the total number of academy visits since enrollment — e.g. Communication (12).
guide.admin.title: Admin
guide.admin.course1text: Go to Admin → Courses. Type the course name in the bottom row of the Courses table and press Add or hit Enter.
guide.admin.course2text: Click the pencil icon to rename a course inline, then save with Enter or the save button. Click the trash icon to delete.
guide.admin.course3text: Below the Courses table, the Enrollments table shows every student enrolled in every course — including who registered them, referral, amount paid, and academy visit count.
guide.admin.perm1text: When a staff member’s PC list is updated, a permission request may appear here. Click Approve to grant access or Reject to deny it.
guide.admin.perm2text: Each auditor card has an Allow All toggle. When on, the auditor can work with any PC. When off, you can add individual PCs to their approved list using the dropdown at the bottom of their card.
guide.tips.title: Tips & Tricks
guide.tips.enterText: In most inline forms (adding a course, editing a name) you can press Enter instead of clicking the Save button.
guide.tips.searchText: The PC list and Academy student list filter as you type — no need to press search or confirm.
guide.tips.redBadgeText: A red remaining-hours badge on a PC means they have used more hours than purchased. Follow up on a new payment.
guide.tips.visitCountText: The number in parentheses next to a course name — e.g. Communication (8) — counts all academy visits since the enrollment start date.
guide.tips.logoutText: Click the logout icon in the top-right header bar. Your session expires automatically after 12 hours of inactivity.
guide.tips.pdfText: Both the Dashboard and the Academy pages have a Print button that generates a polished PDF — great for weekly briefings.
